text.tspan: Why memory speed sets your decode tok/s ceiling.
For each new token, the GPU must:
1. Stream the ACTIVE model weights from memory (dense: full model; MoE: just routed experts)
2. Read the KV cache for the conversation so far (grows with context length)
3. Do one forward pass for that ONE token (compute is tiny vs the memory read)
tok/s ceiling = memory bandwidth / bytes streamed per token
Spark: 273 GB/s . Llama 3.1 8B Q4_K_M = ~4.5 GB of active weights per token (dense)
Measured by Ollama: ~38 tok/s. Gap = KV cache reads, kernel overhead, sampling, runtime cost.
Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B (~6 GB active in Q4) ==> ~45 tok/s ceiling, NOT the 120B's resident size.
That's why a "bigger" MoE can decode faster than a smaller dense model on the same hardware.
Faster tensor cores will not speed up decode much. Faster memory bus will. RTX 4090 (1 TB/s) decodes faster than Spark
on models that fit its 24 GB. Spark trades bandwidth for capacity. Day 4 unpacks quantization; Day 6 unpacks MoE.
